metadata.summary: Catalogue of AI-agent gotchas and test anti-patterns in Rust: Utc::now in assertions, sleep-driven waits, shared lazy_static state, hardcoded ports, mockall predicate hallucinations.

content.title: Rust Testing Anti-Patterns and Agent Gotchas
content.summary: A review checklist and anti-pattern catalogue for Rust test suites, focusing on the failure modes that AI agents introduce most frequently: Utc::now() in assertion paths, sleep-driven async waits, shared global state, hardcoded ports, hallucinated mockall predicates, and flake quarantine misuse.

why.p: AI agents generating Rust tests reliably reproduce the same failure modes: injecting Utc::now() into both production code and assertions (tests pass at noon, fail at midnight), using tokio::time::sleep to "give a task time" (flaky in CI), binding 127.0.0.1:8080 (port collision under parallelism), and inventing mockall::predicate::eq_ignoring_case (compile error). A repeatable review checklist catches these before merge and a lint script catches them before commit.

list.item: Reviewing any Rust test PR authored or modified by an AI agent.
Running the anti-pattern lint script (rust-test-lint.sh) in pre-commit or CI.
During test suite audits on codebases that have grown without a formal testing standard.
When onboarding a new agent that will generate tests for a Rust codebase.
Non-test production code — these checks are scoped to #[test] and tests/ paths only.
Benchmark modules under benches/ — the rules around sleep and Utc::now do not apply there.

section.name: agent-gotchas
section.p: The following are the most common errors AI agents make when generating Rust tests. Each has a concrete detection method and a fix.

antipattern.description: Utc::now() in assertion paths. Agents inject the current timestamp inside the unit under test and inside the assertion. Tests pass at noon, fail at midnight when the day boundary crosses.
antipattern.reason: Use an injected clock (a Clock trait with a mock) or tokio::time::pause to control virtual time. Never compare Utc::now() to Utc::now() in a test assertion.

antipattern.description: sleep-driven async waits. Agents write tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)) to "give the task time to complete". Flaky in CI under load.
antipattern.reason: Use tokio::join!, tokio::time::pause + advance, or a channel/oneshot to wait for completion deterministically. If you need to wait for a spawned task, use a JoinHandle.

antipattern.description: #[tokio::test] flavor mismatch. Default flavor is current_thread. Agents call spawn_blocking or tokio::spawn without specifying flavor = "multi_thread" — the test panics under load because the multi-thread scheduler is not running.
antipattern.reason: Specify #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)] when the unit under test spawns tasks or calls spawn_blocking. Run both flavors at least sometimes to surface Send/Sync bugs.

antipattern.description: Shared lazy_static or once_cell state across test functions without reset. Tests pass locally in isolation, fail under cargo test -- --test-threads=N.
antipattern.reason: Rust tests run in parallel by default. Global mutable state accumulates across tests. Use a per-test fixture struct or initialize state inside each test function.

antipattern.description: Hardcoded ports. Integration tests bind 127.0.0.1:8080; CI runs them in parallel and one fails with "address already in use".
antipattern.reason: Bind to :0 and discover the port with local_addr(). Or use tower::ServiceExt::oneshot which requires no socket at all.

antipattern.description: Hallucinated mockall::predicate APIs. Agents invent predicates like predicate::eq_ignoring_case, predicate::starts_with, predicate::matches_regex that do not exist in the current crate version.
antipattern.reason: Pin the mockall version in Cargo.toml and always verify predicate names against docs.rs/mockall for that version. Use predicate::function(|x| ...) as a fallback for custom logic.

antipattern.description: unwrap() in test assertions making failures unreadable. The next agent or developer cannot determine which operation failed.
antipattern.reason: Replace .unwrap() with .expect("description of what should have succeeded"). The message surfaces immediately in the test failure output.

antipattern.description: Auto-marking flaky tests as #[ignore] without root-cause analysis. Agents flag a flake, mark it ignored, and move on. The hidden bug ships to production.
antipattern.reason: Never auto-ignore a flake. Quarantine it behind a feature flag or a separate CI job, escalate to a human for triage, and remove the quarantine only after the root cause is fixed.

antipattern.description: Doctest on private items. Agents add code blocks to private fn rustdoc; they do not run under cargo test --doc.
antipattern.reason: Promote to a #[test] in the #[cfg(test)] module or to a public example module. Private-item doctests silently provide no coverage.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Drive Rust testing as a four-stage pipeline: (1) a coverage agent reads the crate, lists public functions/methods, and proposes a test inventory categorised as unit, integration, doctest, or property-based; (2) a code-gen agent emits the tests using mockall for traits, proptest for invariants, axum-test / tower::ServiceExt::oneshot for HTTP integration, and testcontainers-rs for DB; (3) a flake agent runs tests under cargo nextest run --partition with seeded randomness and quarantines flakes; (4) a review agent runs the anti-pattern checklist below.
Persist the test stack (nextest, mockall, proptest, testcontainers, axum-test) in .aidocs/product_docs/rust-test-stack.md so successive agents inherit the decisions without re-deriving them.
section.prompt: You are a Rust test architect. For the crate at <path>, list every public fn/method and propose tests: type ∈ {unit, integration, doctest, property}, fixture (mock|real|testcontainer), async (yes|no, runtime flavor), invariants (for property tests). Reject `Utc::now()` or `std::time::*` in assertion paths — propose `tokio::time::pause` or injected clock instead.

Review a Rust test PR. Flag: (1) .unwrap() / .expect() in non-test code paths, (2) std::thread::sleep or tokio::time::sleep used to wait for async, (3) Utc::now() compared in an assertion, (4) #[tokio::test] missing flavor when it relies on multi-threading, (5) shared `lazy_static` state across tests without reset, (6) mock for a type with a trivial real (e.g., chrono, serde_json), (7) integration test that spawns a real network listener on a fixed port (collision risk), (8) doctest on a private item. Cite file:line. No fixes — only flags.

section.name: lint-script
section.p: The following shell script detects the most common anti-patterns statically. Run it in CI or as a pre-commit hook against the repo root.

example.title: rust-test-lint.sh — static anti-pattern scanner
example.code: #!/usr/bin/env bash # rust-test-lint.sh — flag common test anti-patterns set -euo pipefail root="${1:-.}" fail=0 echo "## std::thread::sleep / tokio::time::sleep in tests" grep -rEn '#\[(tokio::)?test' "$root/src" "$root/tests" -A 60 \ | grep -E '(thread::sleep|tokio::time::sleep)\(' && fail=1 || true echo "## Utc::now() in assertion path of a test" grep -rEn '#\[(tokio::)?test' "$root/src" "$root/tests" -A 60 \ | grep -E 'assert.*Utc::now' && fail=1 || true echo "## .unwrap() outside test code" grep -rEn '\.unwrap\(\)' "$root/src" \ | grep -v -E '#\[cfg\(test\)\]|tests/|//' && fail=1 || true echo "## Hardcoded port in integration test" grep -rEn 'bind\(' "$root/tests" \ | grep -E '127\.0\.0\.1:[0-9]+' && fail=1 || true exit "$fail"
example.note: Runs on src/ and tests/ only. Wire as a CI step or a pre-commit hook via .pre-commit-config.yaml with language: script.

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Positive checklist for a well-tested Rust codebase:

list.item: #[cfg(test)] mod tests colocated with code. Black-box tests go to tests/ directory at crate root.
mockall::mock! for traits with side effects. Do not mock pure types; use the real one.
#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)] when the unit under test spawns tasks. Keep current_thread for cheaper tests.
tokio::time::pause for time-sensitive code. Never sleep to wait; advance virtual time.
testcontainers-rs for DB integration tests. Per-test Postgres container or shared with per-test schema; never depend on a shared dev DB.
tower::ServiceExt::oneshot for axum handler tests — drives the router without binding a port.
proptest for invariants of pure functions (parsers, codecs, math). Cheap signal vs hand-written tables.
cargo nextest in CI for parallelism + per-test timeout + flake detection (--retries 1).
Coverage on PRs, not as a gate by itself. cargo llvm-cov --lcov uploaded to Codecov; reviewers eyeball the diff.
Doctest production examples. Top-of-crate //! rustdoc with Examples section that compiles and runs is the cheapest end-to-end test.
Snapshot tests with insta. For complex output (rendered emails, AST), insta keeps tests readable and review-friendly.
Seeded randomness. proptest and any rand use must accept a seed; record failing seeds in CI and replay locally.
Coverage on PRs, not as a gate by itself. cargo llvm-cov --lcov uploaded to Codecov.
Skipping cargo deny/cargo audit in CI is an anti-pattern. Agents add a dependency; security advisory ships next week. Wire into CI.

checklist.check: No Utc::now() in assertion paths — use injected clock or tokio::time::pause.
No sleep calls in async tests — use join!/pause/channel.
Runtime flavor explicitly specified on every #[tokio::test].
No shared lazy_static/once_cell state across test functions.
No hardcoded TCP ports — oneshot or bind to :0.
All mockall predicates verified against docs.rs for pinned version.
All test assertions use expect("message") not unwrap().
No #[ignore] without a linked tracking issue and root-cause comment.
cargo deny and cargo audit wired into CI.
